methodology: Read-only. Replicated detectTableBounds from invoice-image-crop.ts in Python on stored invoice PNG. OCR via deployed extract-invoice (table Pass C prompt). VL historical from raw-runs.json (pre-9f26c0a full-page prompt). Direct OpenAI table-only on identical PNGs not run (OPENAI_API_KEY unavailable locally).

vl_reference.source: layout-meta PROVEN; regenerated from stored invoice

A_crop_differences.conclusion: Implementation crop on stored invoice reproduces VL B_table_only exactly. Crop geometry is NOT the cause of 1/8 accuracy on production path.

vl_historical_B_table_only_run1.prompt: OLD extract-invoice full-page (supplier, invoice_date, total, items)

production_full_stored_image.prompt: Table specialist Pass C only (via extract-invoice)

pre_cropped_vl_or_impl_png_sent_to_edge_.note: detectTableBounds re-runs on 230px input; drops top 17px vs production path

B_ocr_differences.conclusion: VL 100% and implementation are not comparable on prompt alone. With table-only prompt, production path (218-448) fails C/N/G; accidental re-crop path (235-448) succeeds C/N/G in single runs.
B_ocr_differences.confidence: PROVEN for measurements; LIKELY for causal interpretation

prompt_type.detail: VL 100% used full extraction prompt; Pass C uses items-only prompt

effective_ocr_region_when_using_edge_fn.detail: Full-image path sends y=218-448 to table model; pre-cropped input re-detects to y=235-448 (excludes 17px header-margin strip)

table_prompt_on_218_448_region.detail: Same bounds as VL but table-only prompt yields wrong qty/unit_price (reads inner-column/pack values not purchased qty)
table_prompt_on_218_448_region.first_wrong_field: quantity and unit_price on Chocolate, Nata, Gema

ocr_non_determinism.detail: 3 consecutive full-image invocations gave different wrong Chocolate/Nata/Gema values

ordered_divergence_chain.label: Table prompt misreads numeric columns on 218-448 crop
